retrieved.title: Web services
retrieved.text:
The University of Vienna offers a variety of tools for organising your studies. With your [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) all services are available. On this page your will find information about the most important functions of the web services that will accompany you from the first semester onwards:

- [u:space](#c242229)
- [u:find](#c242234)
- [Moodle](#c242239)
- [IT Services for students](#c242247)

u:space, the portal for the organisation of your studies, offers central access to all services that you will require during your studies.

- Apply for admission to a degree programme
- Order your u:card
- Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
- Register for/de-register from courses and examinations
- Check grades and academic progress in the record of examinations
- Download and print your study documents (transcript of records, confirmation of enrolment, student record sheet, etc.)
- [Room details](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/rauminfo) (room type, equipment, capacity, location of the rooms)

For information about registration and the application for admission to a degree programme, please watch our [video tutorials](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/videos) in u:space.

In u:find you can find all courses offered at the University of Vienna (organised according to semesters), as well as all university departments, units and employees.

- Search for courses, examinations, persons or organisations
- Check course and examination dates
- Register for courses and examinations (you will be forwarded to u:space)
- Calendar export
- Building plans of the University of Vienna via Google Maps

retrieved.title: Accessible entrance exam/aptitude test procedure
heading_path: How do I submit my request for special testing accommodations?
retrieved.text:
Submit your application for admission in u:space and pay the administration fee. Select “Submit application.” You will then be registered for the entrance exam procedure/aptitude test.

After you have submitted your application, a window will open in u:space. There you will be informed about the possibility of participating in an evaluation. Further down in the text, you will find a link to the request for accommodations form. Select this link to be redirected to the Servicedesk. Log in to the Servicedesk with your u:account and let us know what accomodations you need. Upload your medical specialist assessments. We also accept assessments of psychotherapists and clinical psychologists.

Do you have questions on requesting special testing accommodations? Please reach out to us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/64/create/1800).

You will receive written notice via e-mail within a few days. Each type of disability is unique. Adaptations to testing will be made on a case-by-case basis. Hence, we will coordinate any special accommodations and their technical implementation with you. We will then inform you about the next steps in the entrance exam/aptitude test procedure.

retrieved.title: Co-registration at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) within the application period. Log into u:space and complete your registration. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at your home university. You need the current student record sheet of your home university.

Submit all required documents as PDF files via the [Service Desk of the University of Vienna](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/744).

You are exempted from pre-approval if one of the following conditions applies:

- You are admitted to a Teacher Education Programme at the University of Applied Arts Vienna or the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna.
- You are admitted to a degree programme that was jointly established by the University of Vienna and your home university.
- You are admitted to a degree programme in cooperation with the University of Vienna ("Kooperationsstudium").

Information about the partial achievements that will be assessed is provided on u:find and during the respective course. Each directorate of studies (in German: Studienprogrammleitung, SPL) determines how places for courses with a limited number of participants and exams are distributed in its degree programmes. The allocation of places will take place either according to the point allocation system, the preference system or the recommended path of study. The status of your registration in u:space informs you whether you are “Registered” (Angemeldet), "On waiting list" (Auf Warteliste) or if there is an "Alternative place available" (Alternativplatz verfügbar). If an Alternative place is available, you can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. If you are registered for a course with continuous assessment and are absent from the first unit without having a good reason, you will be deregistered. Students on the waiting list will be accepted if course places become available. In case you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course, as there may be free places. Examples: Marion attends a seminar (SE) in History. Her presentation, seminar paper and contributions to the discussion of her colleagues’ presentations will be assessed. retrieved.text:
How to apply for a leave of absence:

1. Scan the required documents. Merge them into a PDF file using an online tool of your choice.
2. Complete the online form in the [Service Desk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/742) within the deadline and add the required documents as attachments (at the top of the online form).

After your application has been processed, you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. As soon as the Student's Union fee has been received in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Student's Union fee, you can print out your study sheet under Personal matters > My documents.

retrieved.title: Paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee (= re-enrolment)
heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
retrieved.text: If you study at the University of Vienna (and not any other Austrian educational institution) you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee here each semester. You do not have to complete any other steps. As soon as the prescribed fee has been received, you are re-enrolled in your studies. Then you can download your student documents in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) and validate your [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) (or order and then validate it if it is your first semester). / - Which payment options are available at the University of Vienna? You have four options how you can pay your tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna: 1. Eps-payment via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via eps-payment. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. The eps-payment is only possible with Austrian bank account. 2. Credit card (only Visa and Mastercard) via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via credit card. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. 3. Online banking Use the online banking of your bank. In the info box in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee you can find all data you need

Furthermore, you receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. If you did not gain a place, you may be listed on the waiting list or be offered an alternative parallel course. If students deregister from a course/exam and a waiting list exists, you can move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. In case of long waiting lists, the directorate of studies (SPL) will probably offer additional parallel courses, for which you may register. In this case, you will be able to change the number of points you allocated before a second allocation may start. If you gained an alternative place (remaining place) in a parallel course for which remaining places are available, you have to confirm or reject your place within the deadline defined by the directorate of studies (SPL). If you reject the place, you will be automatically deregistered from the course. The allocated points can not be transferred to the next semester. If you neither confirm nor reject the place, you remain on the waiting list. Related Links: - [Registration

reranked.title: Accessible entrance exam/aptitude test procedure
heading_path: How do I submit my request for special testing accommodations?
reranked.text:
Submit your application for admission in u:space and pay the administration fee. Select “Submit application.” You will then be registered for the entrance exam procedure/aptitude test.

After you have submitted your application, a window will open in u:space. There you will be informed about the possibility of participating in an evaluation. Further down in the text, you will find a link to the request for accommodations form. Select this link to be redirected to the Servicedesk. Log in to the Servicedesk with your u:account and let us know what accomodations you need. Upload your medical specialist assessments. We also accept assessments of psychotherapists and clinical psychologists.

Do you have questions on requesting special testing accommodations? Please reach out to us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/64/create/1800).

You will receive written notice via e-mail within a few days. Each type of disability is unique. Adaptations to testing will be made on a case-by-case basis. Hence, we will coordinate any special accommodations and their technical implementation with you. We will then inform you about the next steps in the entrance exam/aptitude test procedure.

reranked.text:
1. Create your u:account in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) within the application period. Log into u:space and complete your registration. Make sure to remember your login data. You will need your u:account during your studies.
2. Pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at your home university. You need the current student record sheet of your home university.

Submit all required documents as PDF files via the [Service Desk of the University of Vienna](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/744).

You are exempted from pre-approval if one of the following conditions applies:

- You are admitted to a Teacher Education Programme at the University of Applied Arts Vienna or the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna.
- You are admitted to a degree programme that was jointly established by the University of Vienna and your home university.
- You are admitted to a degree programme in cooperation with the University of Vienna ("Kooperationsstudium").

heading_path: I am paying the tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna
reranked.text: If you study at the University of Vienna (and not any other Austrian educational institution) you pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee here each semester. Then you can download your student documents in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en) and validate your [u:card](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en) (or order and then validate it if it is your first semester). / - Which payment options are available at the University of Vienna? You have four options how you can pay your tuition fee/Students' Union fee at the University of Vienna: 1. Eps-payment via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via eps-payment. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. The eps-payment is only possible with Austrian bank account. 2. Credit card (only Visa and Mastercard) via u:space Pay the fee directly in u:space > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Students' Union fee via credit card. Your advantage: You do not have to manually enter the payment information. The payment is booked on the same day. 3. Online banking Use the online banking of your bank. If students deregister from a course/exam and a waiting list exists, you can move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. In case of long waiting lists, the directorate of studies (SPL) will probably offer additional parallel courses, for which you may register. In this case, you will be able to change the number of points you allocated before a second allocation may start. If you gained an alternative place (remaining place) in a parallel course for which remaining places are available, you have to confirm or reject your place within the deadline defined by the directorate of studies (SPL). If you reject the place, you will be automatically deregistered from the course. The allocated points can not be transferred to the next semester. If you neither confirm nor reject the place, you remain on the waiting list. Related Links: - [Registration

selected.title: Web services
selected.text:
The University of Vienna offers a variety of tools for organising your studies. With your [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) all services are available. On this page your will find information about the most important functions of the web services that will accompany you from the first semester onwards:

- [u:space](#c242229)
- [u:find](#c242234)
- [Moodle](#c242239)
- [IT Services for students](#c242247)

u:space, the portal for the organisation of your studies, offers central access to all services that you will require during your studies.

- Apply for admission to a degree programme
- Order your u:card
- Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
- Register for/de-register from courses and examinations
- Check grades and academic progress in the record of examinations
- Download and print your study documents (transcript of records, confirmation of enrolment, student record sheet, etc.)
- [Room details](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/rauminfo) (room type, equipment, capacity, location of the rooms)

For information about registration and the application for admission to a degree programme, please watch our [video tutorials](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/videos) in u:space.

In u:find you can find all courses offered at the University of Vienna (organised according to semesters), as well as all university departments, units and employees.

- Search for courses, examinations, persons or organisations
- Check course and examination dates
- Register for courses and examinations (you will be forwarded to u:space)
- Calendar export
- Building plans of the University of Vienna via Google Maps

[1] Web services
The University of Vienna offers a variety of tools for organising your studies. With your [u:account](https://zid.univie.ac.at/en/uaccount/) all services are available. On this page your will find information about the most important functions of the web services that will accompany you from the first semester onwards:

- [u:space](#c242229)
- [u:find](#c242234)
- [Moodle](#c242239)
- [IT Services for students](#c242247)

u:space, the portal for the organisation of your studies, offers central access to all services that you will require during your studies.

- Apply for admission to a degree programme
- Order your u:card
- Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee
- Register for/de-register from courses and examinations
- Check grades and academic progress in the record of examinations
- Download and print your study documents (transcript of records, confirmation of enrolment, student record sheet, etc.)
- [Room details](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/rauminfo) (room type, equipment, capacity, location of the rooms)

For information about registration and the application for admission to a degree programme, please watch our [video tutorials](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast/videos) in u:space.

In u:find you can find all courses offered at the University of Vienna (organised according to semesters), as well as all university departments, units and employees.

- Search for courses, examinations, persons or organisations
- Check course and examination dates
- Register for courses and examinations (you will be forwarded to u:space)
- Calendar export
- Building plans of the University of Vienna via Google Maps

[2] Planning your semester — This is how it works:
/ - Step 1: Pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee At the beginning of the semester, you have to pay your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee. You can check whether your payment was successful in the study overview on u:space. Once you have paid your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee, you are admitted for the current semester and can register for courses. [Information about tuition fee/Students' Union fee](/en/tuition-fee/) - Step 2: Validate your u:card As soon as you have paid your tuition fee/Students’ Union fee, you can validate your u:card – the University of Vienna’s student ID card – at one of the u:card terminals. Your u:card is then valid for the duration of the current semester. [u:card website](http://ucard.univie.ac.at/en/) - Step 3: Plan your schedule The website on the [University of Vienna's range of degree programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) is a good starting point for planning your schedule. In addition to information about your degree programme, you can also find a link to the curriculum there. The curriculum outlines what achievements you have to complete during your degree programme. With the help of the [semester planner in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/semesterplaner), you can plan your courses and exams for the current semester and create your personal schedule. In the semester planner, you will find a calendar that shows all planned courses and exams for your degree programmes. This allows you to quickly find and resolve overlapping dates. Alternatively,
